---
title: Google Colab Quickstart
description: Run Python in a browser without installing anything.
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this matters

Installation problems can distract beginners from the first goal: running code.
Google Colab lets you start Python in a browser.

[Open a blank Colab notebook](https://colab.new){.colab-button}

::: {.analogy}
### Analogy: a rented kitchen

Colab is like a rented kitchen on the internet. You do not need to buy the oven
or set up the room. You open the kitchen, cook there, and save your recipe.
:::

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning objectives

By the end, you should be able to:

- create a Colab notebook;
- identify code cells and text cells;
- run a Python cell;
- understand that runtime memory can reset;
- save a copy of a notebook for practice.
:::

## Code cells and text cells

A **code cell** runs Python. A **text cell** explains what is happening.
Good notebooks use both.

Try this in a code cell:

```python
print("I am running Python in Colab!")
```

## Runtime state

When you run a cell, Colab remembers variables in the current runtime.

```python
student_name = "Ada"
print(student_name)
```

If the runtime restarts, Colab forgets the variables until you run the cells
again. That is why `Runtime > Restart and run all` is a useful habit.

## Guided practice

::: {.practice-box}
1. Open <https://colab.new>.
2. Add a text cell with the title `My first Python notebook`.
3. Add a code cell that prints your name.
4. Run the cell.
5. Change the message and run again.
:::

## Checkpoint quiz

::: {.quiz-box}
1. What is one advantage of Colab for beginners?
2. What is the difference between a code cell and a text cell?
3. What can happen when the runtime restarts?
:::

<details class="solution">
<summary>Show answers</summary>

1. You can run Python in a browser without installing Python locally.
2. A code cell runs Python; a text cell explains ideas using formatted text.
3. Variables stored in memory disappear, so you should rerun earlier cells.

</details>

## Debugging corner

::: {.debugging-corner}
If Colab says a variable is not defined, ask: "Did I run the cell that creates
this variable? Did the runtime restart?"
:::

## Summary

::: {.lesson-summary}
Colab is the fastest place to begin. Use it to practice small examples, then
later move to VS Code and Jupyter for larger projects.
:::
